name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Hunker down, Hoosiers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
In order to protect our fellow Hoosiers – our neighbors, our family, our friends and our most vulnerable – we’re called on to hunker down. | | |
---|---|---
Friend,

Earlier today, Governor Holcomb spoke directly to Hoosiers, and he had a message for us:

_“I’ve signed another Executive Order that calls on all Hoosiers to **hunker down , stay at home...**unless you’re going out on an essential errand, or essential work or essential business and operations.”_

In order to protect our fellow Hoosiers – our neighbors, our family, our friends and our most vulnerable – we’re called on to hunker down.

But Governor Holcomb also has a message for those Hoosiers out there who are called to serve in these difficult times – who can’t hunker down – our healthcare workers, our grocery store clerks, and all those aiding in the public response:
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ccceaf11e9804339a4270fe64ed16e92&_e=CI0g25Jgh4EiQZzrKrJXDv_Er2m01GunxADahudfXlgk7KjYs868aIFGt1c4V5b0SzwTmOABN2Dh_zUZX2IEWNkReweYuuaL8Z15QStkGASHdVpfNKuwkhs-C-3-ZHov4qzh1L6Rv7aGP_9I2S8DdY8GbXmrvRl4Ldtk0KESzxEpEbk-8UKlSrs3W-nK_qcXPZQevAPPQ77mX82tjlRwKXCkH7jlPH-B4xw83v_SLiaucaVYIHJEfgceQw7i3pnl8To4JLLot0BXYOwkNGASZ-X3eYxwACdMeWIwDJJOudU%3D)
[ **Watch Governor Holcomb's Message and Share on Facebook** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ccceaf11e9804339a4270fe64ed16e92&_e=CI0g25Jgh4EiQZzrKrJXDv_Er2m01GunxADahudfXlgk7KjYs868aIFGt1c4V5b0SzwTmOABN2Dh_zUZX2IEWBDMnWvO2iT_Jf6MVPUzQNU1uKm_izqjQxc77jeHM64GfJLDWY6BTXo5p_9O3oiVF44mupKz9kOge1G1mFEsVLa5Nrj_-f9pEz95lZFjZnio8kGmo-rYHTUZA3YYENne1tg-jLI98Sogke4LxZKzNMmFqKZqn1kuhgd1vpbePpwueGohEhrakzEbAk9LlgAbaOOVeSSX5atUOj3O8PMiJGo%3D)
_**That message is thank you.**_

Thank you to:
* Healthcare workers pulling double shifts.
* General Motors, which is redeploying its Kokomo workforce to produce ventilators.
* Breweries and distilleries that are converting their businesses to produce hand sanitizer.
* Manufacturers donating their protective gear to hospital and the state.
* Businesses that have gone to multiple shifts to spread out their workforce.
* Union shops asking their contractors to drop off the needed items our hospitals.
* Restaurant owners, who are adapting to a new “to-go only” business model.

**If you have questions about Governor Holcomb’s actions, you can:**
* **[Watch Governor Holcomb’s full address here](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ccceaf11e9804339a4270fe64ed16e92&_e=CI0g25Jgh4EiQZzrKrJXDjWNVZj_Lu0yezWv7Ym5NZnNWtPJTvQGVvJfSqxo1k4I-duYcGzF4oqXvvodOTMsM0EWDIi7Z25LO84e8S1GEqFZaYtz7VvkilW9J8Y-Ec0w72aQie4QHFANJ36PaAcQGc2z8Yjjsjob4B8urfdfVOlxhV6XStTqMciqrLS9wpGUxanHW4YXfy1OW2fiOHJEzgU4OfMaJ9yXhcFgVROo7pN6WMVx48EYO127ndJbuKNvQ0HAAJd2rqt9njqkdHIePcbUM93HDerEl4QRNMvpFXjNU6k55PTc-5Ryy08S7oJP0Roiinf1MeSukwk_TQ5bs3Tz6L8okCdFsON1cIYoxWiLuJRhok0m0hBuVDAxMCufd1KWcqa5a5DtLe8jEnXnrJRshn2FYEQ6j_aEFOhuJAdrPLqglB1Edml4179uQ6SC)**
* **[Check out this FAQ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ccceaf11e9804339a4270fe64ed16e92&_e=CI0g25Jgh4EiQZzrKrJXDhIKI1XD15xfxsuZB7C3oeEU2W2xpXPDhBzu9Y7L1Sv66OakjiHZW205a7-4OlIemFLtAE2vgrRXlhJqp12RhwEijPPC1iLYsLqnIaFgk3_gpjqkpuj4rJyli3HkeTA98GE_PnDUYDfQn-y0Ukdm8cZRnL1GGbpuhHtk4hqapDWVedDMn1cmqqBTlVvgL0JWunDXSLpk0vySq3I8Jwc_7rJUTB_1FrYYYPC_4I4gh66rBreZmtKGToty-ckJ5b7EUA%3D%3D)**
* **[Stay up to date on the latest updates from the Indiana Department of Health](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ccceaf11e9804339a4270fe64ed16e92&_e=CI0g25Jgh4EiQZzrKrJXDj2Aq4RaNu2vlVs-Nyl0EkLDpG9ClFrEzESPB_YR0aixUPhhVhd0PnAiRioJWfQUfI1g1Kl94SMLMIhabX5Z86Vk82z5Uxk4FkpoOwG1ez4_R4uMv0iYdVnpZMxTzaDH8U9mMWHQDbXRHbaUCzHN8SnqDmrmPCpg5Erk3p51xJMtrE1nHYZ4gcPPgo9PHFzP4hEZh4dNxsXvPL8bRjWMoI0v77njCI7ZbmaYc6Erni78Z8-6UTP7stjBoEDKl-AQiw%3D%3D)**

And remember, during this time – remember the power we have by each taking action now –

_**“The best thing we can do for each other, for this generation, and for our economy, is to get a handle on the virus by slowing the spread. That’s what we have power over. That’s the power of one, and what you can do. That’s your power. And that’s the power, that together, we’ll remain Indiana strong!”**_

Thanks for all that you’re doing. Together, let’s #HunkerDownHoosiers!

-Your Holcomb Crew

name: Tina Ramirez for U.S. Congress
subject: Press Release – TINA RAMIREZ WINS CHESTERFIELD COUNTY DELEGATE PRE-FILES
body:
|
---
| | |
---
---
| |
---
---
[](https://tinaramirez.cmail19.com/t/r-l-jhdujlc-udthdyhkil-i/)
| | | _For Immediate Release_
March 23, 2020 _**Tina Ramirez Wins Chesterfield County Delegate Pre-Files**_ **Midlothian, Virginia** – Today, the Tina Ramirez campaign announced an overwhelming victory in Chesterfield County in Friday’s delegate pre-file deadline. The Tina Ramirez campaign filed more pre-file delegate forms for the 2020 Republican Convention than any other candidate. The victory in Chesterfield County shows the grassroots movement that has backed Tina Ramirez since the start of her candidacy. Chesterfield is the largest populated county in the 7th District and ultimately the key to taking back Virginia’s 7th Congressional District for Republicans. This victory also shows that Tina Ramirez is the candidate most capable of winning the Richmond Suburbs and beating Abigail Spanberger in the fall. **“I would like to thank the volunteers on the ground that made this overwhelming victory happen,”** said Tina Ramirez. **“Republicans are tired of the same career politicians running and losing for office, they want a conservative businesswoman who will shake up Washington. Our victory in Chesterfield County shows that our campaign of conservative ideas, fresh perspectives, and local grassroots support is winning the suburbs of Richmond. I can’t wait to defend and fight for our conservative values against Abigail Spanberger in the fall.”** ### _Tina Ramirez grew up in rural Powhatan County, Virginia. Tina has 20 years of experience as a public school teacher, foreign policy expert, and authority on international human rights law and religious freedom. Before starting Hardwired Global, Tina worked for the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom, where she developed policy recommendations to protect persecuted communities in Sudan, Iraq, Turkey, Georgia, and Cuba. While working for the U.S. Congress, she founded the bi-partisan International Religious Freedom Caucus. Her intent and major accomplishment was re-directing U.S. policy so that religious freedom was prioritized when dealing with foreign governments. Tina has traveled to over 30 countries in the course of her work and has written and spoken extensively, including before the United Nations and the African Union, and testified before the U.S. Congress. Tina and her 4-year-old daughter, Abigail, live in the Richmond suburbs._ **For Press Inquiries** –
**Contact** : _[Cindy@TinaRamirez.com](mailto:Cindy@TinaRamirez.com)_
**Phone** : 804-384-3674

| This past week has taught me about the incredible courage that Tennesseans have. This pandemic has shown me that our state and our country can come together in difficult times, no matter how divided we might be. ICYMI, because of the outbreak, [I have made the decision to scale down my appearances and campaign events until the virus has slowed its spread](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-j/). My team and I will be working remotely right now. We feel this is the responsible action to take to protect our elderly and most at-risk. I believe how you campaign says a lot about what you'll do when elected. With events postponed across the state, we may be working remotely, but we are not slowing down! Last Thursday, we hosted our first virtual town hall! I really enjoyed answering your questions and discussing COVID-19 with you all. [You can watch the entire town hall here.](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-t/) [This was also the first of many virtual town halls to come while we are social distancing](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-i/). I will be back on Facebook Live for more virtual town halls [Tuesday, March 24](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-d/) and [Thursday, March 26.](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-h/) [Clink the link here to submit your questions!](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-k/) Finally, I know many of you are worried about this virus. While these are tumultuous times, there is no need to panic. Continue to practice social distancing, wash your hands, and enjoy time with your family. I know many of you have questions or are worried. Please feel free to email me directly at manny@drmannyforsenate.com if I can help in any way. **COVID-19 Resources:** There are many resources at your disposal. I urge you to order take-out, donate blood, and above all pray for our country during this time. Also please adhere to the guidelines being put forth by President Trump, Governor Lee, and the[ CDC](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-o/). There are a lot of politicians using this as an opportunity to ask for money, but our friends and neighbors need our help. We've compiled a list of resources below doing some great work. If you can, send them a donation. If you're in need of help right now, reach out to one of those below. And, if you know of others doing great work across our state, send them our way so we can highlight them! One of the most important things any of us can do is to go give blood! Here are some great resources during this pandemic: _Health/Medical Response_
* [Red Cross - Find your local chapter here](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-b/)
* [Direct Relief - Providing doctors and nurses with medical and protective equipment they need to keep treating patients, such as face masks](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-n/)
* [Medic Regional Blood Center - Donate Blood](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-p/)

_Food_
* [Second Harvest Food Bank - Find your local chapter here](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-x/)
* [Meals on Wheels](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-m/)
* [Feeding America](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-c/)

_Community Services_
* [United Way - Find your local chapter here](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-q/)
* [Boys and Girls Club - Providing childcare](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-a/)
* [The Salvation Army](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-f/)
* [The Community Foundation of Tennessee](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-z/)
* [Pandemic Pals](https://mannysethi.cmail20.com/t/r-l-jhhlsit-olhhkyhhid-v/)

Follow us on Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram to keep up with all that we are doing during this pandemic. We aren't slowing this campaign down as we work towards victory in August! Stay safe out there. I'm praying for all of us! Dr. Manny Copyright © 2019 Paid for by Dr. Manny for Senate, All rights reserved.
Our mailing address is:
PO Box 58068, Nashville, TN 37205 |

subject: LIVE Tonight: An Interview with Dr. David Jeremiah
body:
| | Peter, I want to invite you to join me tonight, Tuesday, March 24, at 8PM ET / 5PM PT as I talk LIVE with Dr. David Jeremiah about how we can overcome fear with faith in this time of uncertainty. Dr. Jeremiah is the Senior Pastor of Shadow Mountain Community Church and the Founder of Turning Point, a ministry that reaches millions of people with the Gospel across the globe. He has authored more than eighty books sharing the unchanging Truth of God’s Word with others. As many of us spend time at home during this pandemic, this is a wonderful opportunity to gather together with others across the country to consider specific ways we can live for Christ during this unique moment of our nation’s history. I hope you will gather your family and join us from your living room on [Facebook](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sQ5cX_bEu8cPkWYJ2Mr5-wZKXu3dY5XWtaPM4h3adih&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200324com01&n=1) or [YouTube](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k_GQFnoSEHW1WJEVCcFODMw&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200324com01&n=2) LIVE for my informal conversation with Dr. David Jeremiah. Set an alarm or calendar reminder so you don’t forget, and join me tonight at 8PM ET / 7PM CT / 6PM MT / 5PM PT. Please feel free to share this event with your friends, neighbors, and co-workers. This will be a relevant conversation for everyone during this time. Peter, We have big news about our runoff election. After Gov. Abbott declared a state of emergency, he changed the date of our runoff election in response to the coronavirus outbreak. **Our election has now been moved to July 14th. ** This changes the race but we are still in it for the long haul! Our campaign will focus on supporting the Texas 10 community and local efforts to care for each other. We started this last weekend by raising funds for a food pantry in rural Brenham, Texas. **We all need to work together to get through this crisis. ** We are also in the fight for universal vote-by-mail. Last week our campaign made news by joining with Texas Rep. Sheryl Cole to demand universal access to vote by mail in the upcoming election. We argued that a provision of the Election Code allows every citizen to qualify for a mail-in ballot — to protect our personal health and the health of all Texans. Using our legal theory, the Texas Democratic Party filed suit on Friday against the State of Texas. If they win, everyone in Texas will be able to safely vote from home — and slow the spread. **We will be giving more updates about the work we are doing and amplifying organizations that need our help. This campaign has always been about the people — now we are working hard to keep our community strong and healthy. ** Fighting for you,
Mike Siegel

clean_body: Peter, We have big news about our runoff election. After Gov. Abbott declared a state of emergency, he changed the date of our runoff election in response to the coronavirus outbreak. Our election has now been moved to July 14th. This changes the race but we are still in it for the long haul! Our campaign will focus on supporting the Texas 10 community and local efforts to care for each other. We started this last weekend by raising funds for a food pantry in rural Brenham, Texas. We all need to work together to get through this crisis. We are also in the fight for universal vote-by-mail. Last week our campaign made news by joining with Texas Rep. Sheryl Cole to demand universal access to vote by mail in the upcoming election. We argued that a provision of the Election Code allows every citizen to qualify for a mail-in ballot — to protect our personal health and the health of all Texans. Using our legal theory, the Texas Democratic Party filed suit on Friday against the State of Texas. If they win, everyone in Texas will be able to safely vote from home — and slow the spread. We will be giving more updates about the work we are doing and amplifying organizations that need our help. This campaign has always been about the people — now we are working hard to keep our community strong and healthy. Fighting for you, Mike Siegel ** = PO Box 2409 Austin, TX 78701

It’s difficult to be running a congressional campaign with everything going on in the world now. But what we’re facing today, and the failure of Congress to immediately rise to the occasion, shows just how important it is to elect better representatives. Coronavirus has exposed just how precarious this economy and our healthcare system are for so many Americans. I’m running for office to ensure that no one has to live one missed paycheck away from financial ruin, hunger, homelessness, or illness.
But I need your help. **_[The Democratic Primary is the same date it’s always been, and we’ve got an end-of-quarter fundraising deadline in just a few days.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/5wA/ni0YAA/t.30c/So-7vJAMT-GpUK60uRXWIw/h1/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNI1dByhuImp4gWII3-2BdnJ-2FXFowQXMJiQDx-2FsMYIA4gKeLQLEnAWsDBlAnbDl8uxzbPFe8NUoXEIW0vy1IOM9cdVy6hpm4nXdwgycuX3J-2BZLeyP8V1QoDIZ5wbw0XctfWDJVnGOB5u4vyPELK-2FRy0aF7cxXmnhdRBMnlsd-2BI1FZ0AVWU0kxdhQ5gV1QKCtAjv54Uq5Pr2CqiDTRVuRg121N-2FW7irtVNlw-2F2gudGZRxla-2Fg-3D-3D/DqsF)_** It’s uncomfortable to be asking for money right now, but if we’re going to enact the big changes needed to fix this economy and our healthcare system, we need to elect leaders who prioritize working families over corporations and special interests. **_[If you can, will you make a donation today?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/5wA/ni0YAA/t.30c/So-7vJAMT-GpUK60uRXWIw/h2/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNI1dByhuImp4gWII3-2BdnJ-2FXFowQXMJiQDx-2FsMYIA4gKeNM1xJ4sE4vXGfREafxOCzZdp8i3Ydm2zHCvQLER-2BeCCyh0bVwJxkl8uxhRsNWlHv30B6hvcrBHsEalBmFXhwcCKg44kQsPQRkg7ZZB5jCZiVA8phPHJjGDnwmA9AeUx39jsfdhk6EpygpS8ZENirVSQGwOCTbsFYozLc8JgprkXL7f33jB67uqXx54jgb-2BJKQ-3D-3D/u-i7)_**

Many Americans are being laid off, and that first missed paycheck will put them behind on their mortgage, rent, loans, healthcare bills, and other expenses. But the reality is that no one in the richest nation on Earth should have to live like this.

name: KentuckyforBooker.com
subject: Here’s the next challenge we face:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Our ability to work together and organize a movement of change in the midst of these challenging times is critical if we want to continue forging our path toward a progressive future.
#
After we told our supporters about the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee's (DSCC) endorsement of Amy McGrath — a record number of you rallied to show your support for Charles.
**Thank you:** because of supporters like you we were able to send a strong message that the establishment made a mistake underestimating our campaign and the progressive movement we’ve built.
**But here’s the next challenge we face:** in order to compete with the resources & funding our opponent’s team is getting from the establishment in D.C. — we need to raise a record amount for our upcoming FEC end-of-quarter fundraising goal.
[**Donating even just $5 makes a huge difference as we race to raise $20,000 by next Tuesday. name: Reverend Warnock
subject: From Kayton Homes to Dr. King's pulpit
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
I'm running for Senate to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats. Will you join me?
| | |
---
Friend: In a moment, I'm going to ask you to become one of the 1,000 Founding Members we need in our fight to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats. **But first, let me explain why your support is so important - and what will happen if we fall short.**
I wasn't born with a silver spoon in my mouth. I had 11 brothers and sisters and I grew up in public housing on the west side of Savannah, Georgia. But, from there, I went on to earn a college degree, and worked my way up to serving as the senior pastor at Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.'s church. Now, I have the chance to become the first black senator this state has _ever_ elected.
But the truth is, the opportunities that made my journey possible - from Kayton Homes to Dr. King's pulpit - are growing further and further out of reach for too many people across this country. I'm running for Senate to change that, but I can't win without the help of grassroots supporters like you.
**That 's why I'm asking: [friend, will you chip in before midnight tomorrow to become a Founding Member of our campaign - so we can flip Georgia blue, take back the Senate for Democrats and build a country in which _everyone_ , no matter who you are or where you come from, has a shot at the American dream?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk113-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
[](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk113-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")
The stakes have never been higher: Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight confirms that Democrats can flip the Senate - and analysts at _The Washington Post_ say that winning in Georgia is key to making it happen.
Democrats cannot take back the Senate if we lose this critical battleground state. Plain and simple. That's why Mitch McConnell and the GOP are zeroing in on our race - and why my Republican opponent has already started spending $20 million of her own money against me.
**So please, friend, this is important:[If you care about taking back the Senate for Democrats, I'm counting on you to rush $5 or more right now to become one of the 1,000 essential Founding Members we need by midnight tomorrow to power forward our fight to flip Georgia blue!](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.%3D%3D/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warnock-for-georgia_email?refcode=jtk113-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
Thank you,

Reverend Warnock

name: Team Hutch
subject: WV Votes by Mail--File your Absentee Ballot Application Today
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/79172/images/Honor%20and%20Integrity%201%20copy%20FOR%20NGP.jpg]
The WV Secretary of State announced on Wednesday, March 18th that ALL voters in West Virginia may request an absentee ballot for the May 12th Primary Election . This is being allowed under the State of Emergency issued by the Governor which places limits on the size of public gatherings in an effort to contain the Covid19.
www.tinyurl.com/wvBallot [www.tinyurl.com/wvBallot]
The absentee ballot can be found at this link.
www.tinyurl.com/WVBallot [www.tinyurl.com/WVBallot]
Voters should select the first box in Section 4 as their reason for requesting an absentee ballot, "Illness, injury or other medical reason which keeps me confined." The West Virginia Secretary of State is interpreting the 'shelter in place' rules as medical confinement.
Once you have completed the application, print and fill-out. Then mail, email, or fax to your County Clerk. A list of County Clerks and contact information is found at the link below:
www.tinyurl.com/WVClerks [www.tinyurl.com/WVClerks]
Ballots will begin being mailed this week. The Hutchison Campaign recommends applying for your absentee ballot early so it is received in a timely manner.
If you need further assistance, please contact the WV SOS office at 866.767.8683
Support Justice John Hutchison with a donation today.
www.justicehutch.com/donate [www.justicehutch.com/donate]
Connect With Us:Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/justicejohnhutchison/] Contact Info:Committee to Keep Justice Hutchison
PO Box 5176
Beckley, WV 25801
United States
Twitter [https://twitter.com/JusticeHutch]

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: Checking in... hope you are well
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
| |
---
| | | Friend,

I sincerely hope you and your loved ones are safe and well. It is incredible how quickly things can change from just a normal week to an entirely new version of "normal" we are all adjusting to. Less than two weeks ago my team and I were out touring the 2nd District meeting folks and discussing their concerns for our nation's future. Now we are all being asked to stay home to help curb the spread of COVID-19.

Such a drastic change in the way we interact impacts us all in uniquely different ways, but one thing is certain, it impacts us all. This has caused me to reflect personally on how grateful I am for the things that matter most, particularly my family. My heart and prayers go out to those who are suffering the loss of a loved one or those who are dealing with job loss and other major disruptions in their lives.

As we are all navigating this new territory, I wanted to send a quick note of gratitude for all of you who have reached out in concern for me and my family, and who have been a support to us. I couldn't be more grateful right now to be a Kansan because our state has always been one where people care about their neighbors, and this time in history is no exception.

We may be physically distancing ourselves from one another, but I want you to know that my team and I are always available and will be continuing to brainstorm ways we can connect with you during these changing times. This is hard for all of us in one way or another, but the more we pull together the better we will be.

Here is a website the Kansas Legislature put together with links to available resources for your family or business if you are being impacted by COVID-19 physically, financially or otherwise.
[https://www.kansastogether.com/](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=1748cf38b0&e=1050d051da)

Don't ever hesitate to reach out if there is something I can do for you.

Yours in the fight,

Jake LaTurner

subject: ‘The Pelosi-Schumer Coronavirus Contagion’
body:
"When America most needs bipartisan cooperation, Democrats add to the economic uncertainty by putting their partisan interests above..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8e78612f28&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **_‘The Pelosi-Schumer Coronavirus Contagion’_**

“What a spectacle. Much of America is quarantined at home, the public is so panicked there’s a run on toilet paper, the country desperately wants reassurance, and Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer decide to take a bipartisan rescue bill as a political hostage,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes.

“When America most needs bipartisan cooperation, Democrats add to the economic uncertainty by putting their partisan interests above the needs of the country.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f323016fe0&e=3ee4985eea)

**MORE:** [“List: 74 actions taken by Trump to fight virus and bolster economy”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c620996f91&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “On Monday, right around the time Senate Democrats blocked a $2 trillion coronavirus spending bill for the second time in as many days, we found out House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, fresh from a weeklong vacation, has her own bill, a 1,400-page monstrosity chock full of mandates, regulations, and nonsensical federal programs that leftists would enact if they were king for a day,” John Daniel Davidson writes in [_The Federalist_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=57bcc3eb2f&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “As part of President Trump’s aggressive whole-of-government response, the Department of Defense (DoD) is surging people, capabilities, researchers and equipment to slow the spread of COVID-19 and protect the American people. Like other fights, against other enemies, in other times, we will win,” Defense Secretary Mark Esper writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ad5c3f4abc&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “As soon as the outbreak began, President Trump directed this administration to hit the ground running. In fact, before there was even a single confirmed case in the United States, the VA was already conducting emergency preparedness exercises. And thanks to the president’s decisive leadership, VA facilities have enough supplies to handle an influx of patients,” Veterans Affairs Secretary Robert Wilkie writes in the [_Military Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8516d7a491&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7bc028b73e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9d0ae759a9&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9c647daf0f)
---

name: Mark Warner
subject: Standing together for Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
One of the most critical things we can do right now to advance the mission of creating a better Virginia for all is to take care of our communities.
|
---
| | Derek --

I'm writing to check in with everyone on this team today, because I know that the spread of coronavirus is putting a huge strain on Virginians: mentally, physically, and financially. There's no doubt that this is a trying time for our state and our nation. I know that Virginians are tough and resilient -- we will make it through this together. The stories I've heard over the last few weeks of people across the Commonwealth organizing to help others give me hope in this uncertain time.

One of the most critical things we can do right now to advance the mission of creating a better Virginia for all is to take care of our communities. There are folks in our communities who are sick, or taking care of a sick relative. There are folks who are working double shifts at the hospital to treat those with coronavirus, and folks who are working overtime to keep our grocery stores and pharmacies running smoothly. **There are folks who could use our help right now.**

Governor Northam announced that Virginia schools will be closed through the rest of the academic year, which means that children who are already food insecure could be at higher risk of missing meals. Food insecurity in the face of coronavirus is a growing problem in Virginia, and one of the best things we can do to combat it is by supporting food banks. Sen. Kaine and I are urging the Food and Nutrition Service (FNS) and the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) to operate a Disaster Food Distribution Program. This would ensure the safe and efficient distribution of food to families in need. **Until this is in place, there are other ways to help.**

**[If you have the means, please consider supporting your community with a contribution to the Federation of Virginia Food Banks, which serves the entire Commonwealth.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d57f/53d9897e/532abc2a/1c89994e/2604321306/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjUyMSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-covidfoodbank?refcode=20200324_f_1_fl__521__&refcode2=ask1&amount=5")** **[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d57f/53d9897e/532abc2a/1c89994d/2604321306/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjUyMSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-covidfoodbank?refcode=20200324_f_1_fl__521__&refcode2=cont&amount=5")** If you need more information on how to prepare for and protect yourself from coronavirus, please visit **[the Center for Disease Control (CDC)'s dedicated site.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d57f/53d9897e/532abc2a/1c89994c/2604321306/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.coronavirus.gov/")**

Thank you for your support,

Mark Warner
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Janet=20Barresi?=
subject: A word of encouragement
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/d2f3a8915b7f/calling-all-patriots-12262702?e=795c6d9796)
---
| |
---
| | Peter,
**"Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous hand."
\- Isaiah 41:10**

Such a sweet reminder in these difficult times. Often times we find ourselves struggling to grasp a full understanding of God's plan for us and our country. It is imperative that we remember that He is in control, always.

I know this is a difficult time for all of us - especially our small businesses. As of Monday, Oklahoma has 81 confirmed cases of COVID-19. Most restaurants and entertainment venues in Oklahoma City and surrounding communities have closed. I have always been an advocate of small business and want to urge you as best as you can safely to “buy local." For shops, that may mean buying a gift certificate online if you can. As for our restaurants, treat yourself to takeout orders, food delivery or gift certificates.

As far as medical help, if you or a loved one are not feeling well, here is a very helpful visual resource to help diagnose your symptoms.
---
|
---
| On that note, I ask that we remember our healthcare workers and first responders on the frontlines of the Coronavirus epidemic. Let's all send up some extra prayers for them. And if you have a friend or family member in the medical profession, send them a message of encouragement and appreciation. This is, without a doubt, one of the most difficult times of their career.

And if you're one of our healthcare workers, be advised that the YMCA of Greater Oklahoma City is offering daily, weekday child care to parents who are first responders or work in health care, core services or the Oklahoma State Department of Health who are required to work and do not have access to other care amid the COVID-19 coronavirus crisis.

I have no doubt we will make it through this together and come out on the other side better and stronger as a state and as individuals.

Lifting you and your family up in prayer for your safety health and well-being. Take care and talk to you soon.

Best,
\--Janet Barresi
---

name: Nina Ahmad
subject: IMPORTANT ANNOUNCEMENT FROM NINA
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/SrK8_fNQlxo4P2XTcuP7kG-lEjOjIOTSTmeLsNLKDbE=/6PeBgcl_MIevh5rNEGLoByv4sYLnRV80s54_W6A6wog=)
March 24, 2020
Dear Friends and Supporters:
As you know, with the spread of COVID-19 (Coronavirus), a lot is changing rapidly in our lives and communities. Top of mind for so many of us is ensuring that our families and communities remain safe, engaged, and informed to set the best course of action.
As many of you know, from my own personal history, I believe strongly in the human capacity for concern and resiliency, so each of us pitches in and rises to meet even the deepest hardships.
With so many questions on COVID-19 still facing us, I have re-oriented my campaign to listen and advance resources, so accurate and timely information is being shared.
I plan to leverage my own network and experiences as a scientist in the medical community in the coming weeks and have asked those I know to do the same.
**With this in mind, please join me in a special virtual event our team has organized this Thursday, March 26 at 7pm.**
**[](https://act.myngp.com/el/SrK8_fNQlxo4P2XTcuP7kG-lEjOjIOTSTmeLsNLKDbE=/BKYh5unxl52Dj2bzljdP-dCEk4ZO4L2JbS_4Z5HKXEk=)**
I invite you to join myself, Congressman Brendan Boyle, Congressman Dwight Evans, Former Philadelphia Commissioner of Health Dr. Walter Tsou, Dr. Ellen Tedaldi, community members and labor leaders **at 7pm ET **as we host a Tele-Townhall to frame a community dialogue around COVID-19.
You can go-to **[ninaforpa.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/SrK8_fNQlxo4P2XTcuP7kG-lEjOjIOTSTmeLsNLKDbE=/BKYh5unxl52Dj2bzljdP-dCEk4ZO4L2JbS_4Z5HKXEk=)** to sign-up or email me direct on this message and provide your phone number so we can add you to the invite list. You will receive a call shortly before the event begins, so be on the look-out.
We will provide best practices, links to resources, and real time updates to better inform the public from health experts and respected policymakers. We hope to hear from you and hope you will join us. **Please stay safe.**
Warmest regards,

Paid for by Nina for PA
---

Philadelphia PA 19107 United States

As many of you know, from my own personal history, I believe strongly in the human capacity for concern and resiliency, so each of us pitches in and rises to meet even the deepest hardships. With so many questions on COVID-19 still facing us, I have re-oriented my campaign to listen and advance resources, so accurate and timely information is being shared. I plan to leverage my own network and experiences as a scientist in the medical community in the coming weeks and have asked those I know to do the same. With this in mind, please join me in a special virtual event our team has organized this Thursday, March 26 at 7pm. = I invite you to join myself, Congressman Brendan Boyle, Congressman Dwight Evans, Former Philadelphia Commissioner of Health Dr. Walter Tsou, Dr. Ellen Tedaldi, community members and labor leaders at 7pm ET as we host a Tele-Townhall to frame a community dialogue around COVID-19. You can go-to = to sign-up or email me direct on this message and provide your phone number so we can add you to the invite list. You will receive a call shortly before the event begins, so be on the look-out. We will provide best practices, links to resources, and real time updates to better inform the public from health experts and respected policymakers. We hope to hear from you and hope you will join us. Please stay safe. Warmest regards, Paid for by Nina for PA Nina for PA PO Box 40681 Philadelphia PA 19107 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please =.

Hi there, I wanted to check in with you real quick. This is a difficult time for so many of you and your loved ones, and I’ve been thinking non-stop about how this crisis is affecting all of you. It’s okay to be worried, angry, scared, or whatever other emotion you may be feeling right now. Our seniors and folks who are immunocompromised are concerned about their health and safety. People are losing their jobs. Small businesses are closing their doors. And families are worried about what happens next. name: Congressman Al Green
subject: Healthcare workers are our front line
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/Dq13IVSVzGh22wnKOiWaAP0Nm15yu0f9OLm9-y1Q-XQ=/Vc3HAI3pFyKcOe-eqqy13sOiK7r0br3I3hfYsL-iEOg=)
---
| Folks, **As COVID-19 continues to spread, our healthcare workers are our front line of defense.** Nurses, doctors, and paramedics are risking their health and safety every day to treat patients and contain this virus -- and we owe them the ability to protect themselves on the job. Unfortunately, many hospitals and healthcare centers around the country are experiencing a shortage of PPE masks (personal protective equipment). Without these masks, healthcare professionals are at high risk of contracting COVID-19 while working with potential patients -- and spreading the infection to their families, neighbors, and coworkers. **Good news: We were fortunate to find 8,000 PPE masks to be distributed to healthcare professionals in my home city of Houston.** My team and I have been searching for masks for weeks now, and we were lucky to be able to secure a few thousand. We will continue to search for more masks and other supplies to keep healthcare workers safe. In the meantime, I am working around the clock to combat the spread and effects of COVID-19. Last week, my colleagues in the House -- Democrat and Republican -- joined together to pass an emergency relief package to provide free testing, accessible healthcare, and direct paychecks to Americans whose lives have been upended by this pandemic. Rest assured, we are working hard to turn that bill into law as soon as possible. And finally, my most sincere thank you to the healthcare officials, grocery store workers, and civil servants who are keeping us all safe and informed in these trying times. We cannot thank you enough. In service, Congressman Al Green _We have a lot of work to do to keep Al in Congress and we need your help. Chip in now to help keep Al fighting for us._

| **[DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/Dq13IVSVzGh22wnKOiWaAP0Nm15yu0f9OLm9-y1Q-XQ=/1nNqrIAJyu2qu4wu8WEQdR2LgKcL1GqzgMTJTPB2Jq8=?refcode=E1897-20200324-PPE-SB&amount=25)**

subject: Ways you can help.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
_****_
The best thing you can do right now to help curb the spread of coronavirus is to social distance at home, wash your hands frequently, and avoid touching your face. **However, if you find yourself wanting to do more, here's a simple list of ways to be of service during this crisis:**
**1\. Donate blood. **There is currently a nationwide blood shortage due to the coronavirus pandemic. If you are a healthy candidate for blood donation, we urge you to find your nearest Red Cross blood donation center.
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/cbOCi248N_RueZHhqdcmzAFX41t2Md6hZFMbOJ8ql8U=/GZOWDWxoFYeCX6tZsKviFN3qJ4141R6u9vkWbW4cgUo=)
**2\. Donate personal protective equipment (PPE). **Hospitals across the country are in desperate need of PPE. If you have access to unused N95 respirator masks, rubber gloves, face shields, disposable booties, safety goggles, or hospital gowns, please consider donating yours to local hospitals. Right now, the PPE needs of healthcare professionals far outweigh our own. The link below will direct you to PPE drop off locations across California.
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/cbOCi248N_RueZHhqdcmzAFX41t2Md6hZFMbOJ8ql8U=/LaGqa8SjSZubT0Jlm5KdloalBjxF-V_OTb11HdiOVAE=)
**3. Make a donation to your local food bank.****** Food banks across the country are feeling the pressure due to the demands of coronavirus. They could use volunteers and donations.
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/cbOCi248N_RueZHhqdcmzAFX41t2Md6hZFMbOJ8ql8U=/awsPBPlqFfEooyDC6Kf0A-HRvcfn6qCStci6ff6AMck=?df_id=2541&mfc_pref=T&2541.donation=form1&NONCE_TOKEN=6FB18C1681D472C3B0685E3E96D49780)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/cbOCi248N_RueZHhqdcmzAFX41t2Md6hZFMbOJ8ql8U=/B6DN-45JbvHU_DmxF-Q0dD2Tb8VTycJBArzM5KulyOM=)

Thank you and stay well,
\-- _Team S anchez_

body:
Will you take my brief health care survey to let me know what you think we should do to fix the system?

|

[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903ea/7f51f7bc/4c390323/4ef1efca/2657243697/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Now more than ever, Peter, health care is at the front of every Texan's mind. We've been talking for months about how Texas is the most uninsured state in the country, and how we need to implement a public option so that everyone can get the health care they need.

**And right now we 're seeing the cracks in our health system and how wide they become when we face a crisis. [Will you take my brief health care survey to let me know what you think we should do to fix the system?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903ea/7f51f7bc/4c390323/4ef1efc9/2657243697/VEsE/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://mjfortexas.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/mj-hcsurvey")

Yesterday was the 10th anniversary of the passage of the Affordable Care Act**, and despite Sen. Cornyn's attempts to eliminate it, it's still standing strong. It's still protecting people with pre-existing conditions, allowing young adults to stay on their parents' health care plans until 26, and expanding access to coverage for people across America.

**But it 's clear we need to do more. I want to know what you think about how we can best improve our health care system right now. Will you take my brief survey about the future of health care in America?** **[**TAKE THE SURVEY**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903ea/7f51f7bc/4c390323/4ef1efc9/2657243697/VEsF/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://mjfortexas.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/mj-hcsurvey")** We've seen just how unprepared our health care system is for a massive pandemic like COVID-19. It's obvious that change is necessary to protect ourselves and ensure that everyone has the quality, affordable care they deserve. **We can 't fix our health care without leaders who listen to us, and put public health before the special interests.**

\-- MJ

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903ea/7f51f7bc/4c390323/4ef1efc8/2657243697/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExMTEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200324.1d__1111__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

With many businesses struggling because of the COVID-19 outbreak, we must do whatever we can to support the local and small businesses in our community. subject: President Trump is standing up for working families in this crisis
body:
While Democrats in Congress stall on economic relief for American workers and businesses, the President is standing up for all... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=97789e8c5d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 24, 2020
---
| | |
# **President Trump is standing up for working families in this crisis**

**While Democrats in Congress**[stall on economic relief](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4a1798229a&e=3ee4985eea) for American workers and businesses, the President is standing up for all those affected by this global pandemic.

**Today, President Trump** and Vice President Mike Pence held a “virtual townhall” with Fox News from the White House. While answering questions from citizens, the President put Democrat leaders on notice: Stop holding Senate negotiations hostage for a partisan wish list that has nothing to do with Coronavirus or protecting our economy.

** WATCH:** [Democrats want a Green New Deal. I want to help American families.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d78ef2cd50&e=3ee4985eea)

**Speaker Nancy Pelosi** and Minority Leader Chuck Schumer are putting their donor base first. They want a relief package that has “nothing to do with the workers—that had to with an agenda that they’ve been trying to get passed for 10 years,” President Trump said.

**America can’t afford** politics as usual during this crisis. President Trump has understood that from the beginning, which is why his Coronavirus response reflects bipartisan leadership and not a checklist of partisan pork like the Democrats’ bill. In just the past few days, he and his Administration:

* Signed an order to [prevent hoarding and price-gouging](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6988b7635f&e=3ee4985eea) of crucial supplies

* Approved multiple major disaster declarations for impacted areas

* Partnered with America’s top tech companies to [unleash supercomputing resources](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9ecc746985&e=3ee4985eea) to fight the virus

* Paved the way for low-interest disaster loans to [help small businesses](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8776faf66f&e=3ee4985eea)

* Moved Tax Day for all Americans from April 15 to July 15

**All of these actions** , of course, are aimed squarely at responding to this global pandemic and protecting American families. That’s because President Trump is taking this crisis seriously—both the public health threat and the economic pressure facing middle-class Americans. Democrats in Congress must do the same before it’s too late.

🎬 [Small businesses have President Trump’s full support!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c73e9218a1&e=3ee4985eea)

**VP Pence:**[It is crucial for Congress to come together](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b44c966a8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **🎥****How to protect your loved ones**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b265a18265&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Americans sprang into action** last week in response to President Trump’s call to help slow the spread of Coronavirus. Millennials and other young Americans are doing their part, and many want to know how else they can help.

**White House Coronavirus Response** Coordinator Dr. Deborah Birx says there are important ways that young people can contribute.

**“The way** **the Millennials** can help us is help your parents and grandparents understand each of these issues,” she said. “Help them get connected by media or phones or videoconferencing so they don’t have to leave their houses.”

**Of course,** continuing to avoid crowds is key. “You’re the group that’s most likely to be [spreading the] virus without knowing it,” she added.

**Public health experts** have shared other ways that everyone can do their part:

* 🎬 [How to protect our most vulnerable](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f3ca9a9e3&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 [An explainer on social distancing](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b851182268&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 [Donating blood is safe—please contribute!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d56eae5a99&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 [If you feel sick, stay home](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=66a5f557c7&e=3ee4985eea)

---
